description: Figure 5 shows the dependence of saturation magnetization 4πJ_s (in Gauss) on carbon content (0.8–1.06 wt%) for Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steels with fixed 9% Al and 30% Mn, aged at temperatures between 350°C and 550°C for 16 h (dashed curves) and 100 h (solid curves). The plot comprises ten curves labeled by aging temperature and duration. For 100 h aging, 4πJ_s increases markedly with carbon content and saturates above approximately 0.92–0.96 wt% C, reaching plateau values near 2200–2400 G, with the 400°C/100h, 450°C/100h and 500°C/100h conditions displaying the highest magnetization. For 16 h aging, magnetization rises with both carbon content and aging temperature, remaining near zero at 350°C but reaching approximately 1600–1800 G at 550°C. At 550°C, the 16 h and 100 h curves nearly converge, indicating that kappa-phase precipitation kinetics are accelerated at this temperature. The caption and citation context indicate that carbon, as the most diffusion-mobile element, is the leading component at the onset of aging, and the measured saturation magnetization provides qualitative information on the intensity of kappa-phase precipitation during austenite aging.
